name: Cicada
summary: The periodical cicada is a unique insect famous for its extremely long life cycle, spending 17 years underground as a nymph before emerging in massive...
metaTitle: Cicada Facts | Habitat, Diet, Buzzing Sound & Photos
metaDescription: Cicada facts for kids with photos, forest habitat, sap diet, loud buzzing sound, 17-year underground life cycle, and predators on one easy-to-read page.
behaviors: emerging from ground in massive swarms
adaptations: clear membrane wings with strong veins
funFacts: Periodical cicadas live underground as nymphs for a whopping 17 years, sucking fluid from tree roots, before coming out all at once!
The loud buzzing sound of cicadas is made only by males, using special vibrating drum-like organs on their stomachs called tymbals to attract females.
Cicadas are the loudest insects in the world, with their songs reaching up to 100 decibels—as loud as a lawnmower or a chainsaw!
When they emerge, they leave behind their hollow, crunchy brown skins (exoskeletons) clinging to tree bark and fences.
By emerging all at once in billions (a strategy called predator satiation), cicadas overwhelm predators, ensuring plenty of them survive to lay eggs.
Adult cicadas do not chew leaves; they have a needle-like straw mouth that they use to poke into twigs and drink sweet tree sap.

faq.question: What is a periodical cicada?
faq.answer: A periodical cicada is an insect famous for spending 17 years living underground as a nymph before emerging in massive swarms to live as a flying adult for a few weeks.

faq.question: Where do periodical cicadas live?
faq.answer: They live in the deciduous forests and woodlands of the eastern and central United States.

faq.question: What do cicadas eat?
faq.answer: They are herbivores that drink sap. Nymphs drink fluid from tree roots underground, while adults drink sap from tree branches and twigs.
